issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken — content not hidden visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static instead of position: absolute, with width: auto and height: auto instead of 1px dimensions. This means content marked as screen-reader-only will NOT be visually hidden — it will display normally on screen, confusing all users and defeating the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none but provides no visible replacement (no box-shadow, no border, no non-zero outline). Keyboard users relying on focus indicators will not be able to see which element has focus, blocking navigation entirely.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with no visible replacement (no box-shadow, no border). Button elements are critical interactive elements — keyboard users cannot identify which button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0 and no visible replacement. Search inputs are frequently used — keyboard users cannot see when the search field has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The form input :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with no visible replacement. Form inputs are critical for data entry — keyboard users cannot identify which field has focus, blocking form completion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below 24px minimum — .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px WCAG 2.2 minimum for touch targets. Users with motor impairments or using touch screens may fail to activate small buttons reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below 24px minimum — .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px WCAG 2.2 minimum. Icon-only buttons are already hard to identify — small size compounds the accessibility barrier.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below 24px minimum — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px WCAG 2.2 minimum. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small checkboxes accurately.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override — .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 450 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the skip link animate into view.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override — .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform: perspective() rotateY() rotateX() with transitions, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 764-767 has empty rule blocks. The 3D transform motion will still occur for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override — .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) with no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see this card animate on hover.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. This impairs readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision who rely on clear word boundaries.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — comparison table "Not included" cells
issuesFound.explanation: The td[aria-label="Not included"] and td[aria-label="Not available"] cells use colour: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) on a white or near-white background. This produces approximately 1.6:1 contrast ratio, far below the 3:1 minimum for UI components. Users with low vision cannot distinguish these cells.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. Most links rely on colour alone (#5588bb) to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1. Users with colour blindness or low vision cannot identify links.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled open and closed via the open() and close() functions, but these functions only manipulate CSS classes and body scroll lock state. They never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state of the drawer to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but these functions only toggle the hidden attribute and CSS classes on the panel. They fail to set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button that controls the panel.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input implements a combobox pattern where the suggestion listbox is shown and hidden via _open() and _close() functions. These functions manage the internal _searchState.open boolean but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the input element to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selector buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) act as toggle buttons that visually indicate an active state via CSS classes, but the function does not update the aria-pressed attribute to reflect the pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (inside _periodBtns.forEach, after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive)): 
    setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the markInvalid function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, which is required to communicate the invalid state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl)): 
    field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Wizard progress bar missing ARIA progressbar attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step wizard uses a progress indicator element (queried as [data-wizard-progress]) but does not assign role="progressbar" or the required aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax attributes, making the progress value announced by aria-valuenow meaningless without context.

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the empty state may show, but these dynamic changes are not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware that the content has changed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (inside filterAnimation.run callback, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches)): 
    liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (e.g., from monthly to annual), the price values update visually and via aria-label, but the fact that the period itself has changed is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the context change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (after _updateSavingsMessage(period)): 
    liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users activate the scroll-to-top button, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves to the skip link, but there is no announcement confirming the action, which can be disorienting for screen reader users who may not realize the page position has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (after setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }, 300)): 
    liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving the field marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markValid, after the if block handling aria-describedby): 
    field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button starts in a closed state when the page loads, but the init function does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial state on page load.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced via live region

issuesFound.fix: Line 3267: Ensure `_countBadge` has `aria-live="polite"` and `aria-atomic="true"` set, or use `liveRegion.announce()` to speak the count: `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing price changes not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
